observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fast track the procurement process of the housing materials for the immediate utilization of the financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: Management augmented the assistance in an amount of ₱300,000.00 for the procurement of housing materials for the beneficiaries.
observations.reason: The procurement of housing materials was delayed due to failed biddings. Bid opening was scheduled to be on March 17, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, Management coordinate   with accredited agencies/trainers for the conduct of and attendance to trainings of key officials of the LGU to sustain continuous capacity building pursuant to Item 4.1.A.3 of JMC No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Management appropriated funds for trainings and seminars on GAD by employees. Management also planned to invite resource speakers for the conduct of their capacity building programs.
observations.reason: No trainings were attended or conducted.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We have recommended and Management agreed to conduct an
annual inventory taking for all the property owned/controlled by the
Municipality; and to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
Property and Equipment in accordance with the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2020-006.

2.8 We have recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal
Accounting Office maintain a special account in the General Fund for
the agricultural enterprise; prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers
for each program of the enterprise; and prepare financial reports at
the end of the year for each special account, in order to provide
management and users adequate information in evaluating and
monitoring the efficiency and effectiveness of the programs.

3.7 We have recommended and Management agreed to:

a. include in the loan application form the dates and receipt of
loan application, amount of the loan, and other relevant and
necessary information that would help in the evaluation and
assessment of the creditworthiness of the loan applicant;

b. indicate in the promissory note the date, the term and maturity
date; and

c. monitor the implementation of the program from which
purpose the loan was granted in order that courses of action be
instituted immediately for the success and sustenance of the
livelihood program.

4.4 We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
reclassify the loans receivable with the following adjusting entry:

Loans Receivable – Others ₱588,820.00
Loans Receivable - Local Government Units ₱588,820.00

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the remaining medicines and medical supplies funded by the BGCM with the following adjusting entry: 
Drugs and Medicines for Distribution   ₱93,078.60    

Drugs and Medicines Inventory       ₱93,078.60   

We have recommended further and Management agreed to prepare a separate inventory and monitoring report for medicines and medical supplies which are intended for distribution.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant made the adjusting entries in the LGU's monthly report for August 2021.








Separate inventory and monitoring report were prepared.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management ensure that cash advance for salaries, wages, honoraria, incentives and assistance be supported with a duly approved payroll showing the list of payees and the corresponding net amount each payee should receive in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Cash advances for salaries, wages, honoraria, incentives, and assistance were  supported with an approved payroll.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended  that Management expedite the transfer and registration of the above-mentioned motor vehicles as government-owned with the LTO and subsequently mark these vehicles with “For Official Use Only” and with the name of the LGU pursuant to COA Circular No. 75-6 and the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles.
observations.managementAction: All vehicles were marked "for Official Use Only". Four vehicles were already transferred and registered under the name of the LGU.
observations.reason: Transfer and registration of three vehicles under the name of the LGU still on process.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management include a clause in the purchase order making reference to the general and specific conditions of the contract pursuant to the provisions of the Procurement Manual for LGUs, Volume II; and

Henceforth, Management agreed to notify the Office of the Auditor within 24 hours after acceptance of deliveries pursuant to the provision of COA Circular No. 95-006.
observations.managementAction: Management used the prescribed Purchase Order Form.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management include a clause in the purchase order making reference to the general and specific conditions of the contract pursuant to the provisions of the Procurement Manual for LGUs, Volume II; and

Henceforth, Management agreed to notify the Office of the Auditor within 24 hours after acceptance of deliveries pursuant to the provision of COA Circular No. 95-006.
observations.managementAction: Management did not notify the COA Auditor of deliveries within 24 hours after acceptance.
observations.reason: Management will notify the COA Auditor within 24 hours after acceptance of goods delivered.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management prepare and submit to the COA Auditor the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels, for evaluation and verification purposes, pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 on the rules and regulations governing the use of government motor vehicles and fuel consumption.
observations.managementAction: Monthly report of Fuel Consumption were prepared.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management ensure that DTTs are properly and completely accomplished before a vehicle can depart from the garage/motorpool, as provided for in Annex A of COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977.
observations.managementAction: Driver's Trip Tickets were properly accomplished before departure from station.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management reconstitute the Municipality’s Inventory Committee who shall prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and undertake the physical inventory of its PPE pursuant to COA Circular No.  2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: Office order was issued to reconstitute the Municipality's Inventory Committee. Inventory tags were already procured.
observations.reason: Management did not yet conduct physical inventory of properties and equipment. Reiterated in Finding No. 1 of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management submit justification/validation reports for the approval of contract time extensions due to adverse weather conditions; and require the contractor   to facilitate completion of the project by providing additional manpower in order for the beneficiaries to enjoy the timely utilization and enjoyment of the benefits that could be derived therefrom.



We have recommended that henceforth, Management require the Project Engineer to strictly monitor the implementation of all infrastructure projects to ensure that these are satisfactorily completed within the specified target completion dates.
observations.managementAction:
Management required contractors to submit justifications for their request for contract time extensions and require contractors to facilitate completion of projects.







Project engineers were assigned  to closely monitor ongoing projects.
observations.reason:
Ten projects under Cluster 1-2020, Cluster 4-2020 and Cluster 5-2020 are still ongoing.







Some projects are still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management monitor the implementation of the project and require the contractor to fast track its completion so that the intended beneficiaries may enjoy the optimum benefits that could be derived from the project.

We recommended that Management immediately return to the Source Agencies the unspent balances of its Due to NGAs account and NDRRMC pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 94-013 and 2014-002 and refund the unutilized PDAF balances to the National Treasury in accordance with Supreme Court Decision GR Nos. 208566, 208493 and 209251.
observations.managementAction: Checks were remitted to the source agencies.
